name: 1781 Club
about: The 1781 Club is a private country club located in the scenic hills of Western Pennsylvania, offering a full range of amenities including an 18-hole golf course, dining options, and event spaces. The venue combines traditional club values with modern facilities, featuring both on-course play and indoor golf capabilities. It is designed to build community through thoughtful membership offerings while also welcoming public golfers to enjoy the course and facilities.
why_golfers_like_it: Scenic 18-hole course in the scenic hills of Western Pennsylvania
Modern indoor golf facilities alongside traditional play
Cash-free facility accepting all major credit/debit cards
Combines traditional golf with supper club-inspired dining

food_and_drink.notes: Farmers Table Lounge & Restaurant serves seasonal, local and fresh ingredients
Full bar service available in dining facilities

faq.question: Do I need clubs?
faq.answer: The venue offers both traditional golf course play and indoor golf facilities; club requirements would depend on the specific activity chosen.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, the 1781 Club is a cash-free facility that requires all food and beverages to be purchased on-site, accepting all major credit/debit cards.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The venue doesn't specify exact play times, but standard 18-hole golf typically takes 4-5 hours depending on course conditions and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the 1781 Club appears to be family-friendly, offering membership programs that emphasize 'family and friendship' as part of their tradition.
